summary: Wickes launches £10m share buyback programme to reduce capital and cancel shares
body: Commenced a third share buyback programme worth up to GBP10.0m. Buyback will end on or before 31 December; purpose stated as reducing share capital with intent to cancel all repurchased shares. Appointed Investec Bank PLC to conduct the programme. Company can repurchase up to 16.5m shares, under authority granted at its AGM. Current stock price referenced: 222.10p, +6.0% on Wednesday; 12-month +25%.

summary: Wickes launches £10m share buyback programme to reduce capital by end of year
body: Commenced a third share buyback programme worth up to GBP10.0m. Buyback will end on or before 31 December; purpose stated as reducing share capital with intent to cancel all repurchased shares. Appointed Investec Bank PLC to conduct the programme. Company can repurchase up to 16.5m shares, under authority granted at its AGM. Current stock price referenced: 222.10p, +6.0% on Wednesday; 12-month +25%.

summary: Capricorn extends takeover bid deadline to 6 May amid multiple competing proposals
body: Extended the deadline for Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah for Trading LLC to announce a firm offer or walk away (per UK takeover rules). Previous deadline would have expired at 5pm London time “today”; new deadline set to 6 May. Extension rationale: gives the bidder (member of the Cafani Group) additional time to progress funding arrangements “as requested”. Capricorn says it has received multiple unsolicited takeover proposals. Capricorn had provided due diligence access earlier this year after evaluating multiple proposals. Share price reaction referenced: +3.2% to 325.00p on Wednesday afternoon.

summary: Capricorn Energy extends takeover deadline to 6 May for Cafani Group bid
body: Extended the deadline for Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah for Trading LLC to announce a firm offer or walk away (per UK takeover rules). Previous deadline would have expired at 5pm London time “today”; new deadline set to 6 May. Extension rationale: gives the bidder (member of the Cafani Group) additional time to progress funding arrangements “as requested”. Capricorn says it has received multiple unsolicited takeover proposals. Capricorn had provided due diligence access earlier this year after evaluating multiple proposals. Share price reaction referenced: +3.2% to 325.00p on Wednesday afternoon.

summary: Martin Cooke steps down as independent non-executive director on 30 April
body: Independent non-executive director Martin Cooke to step down 30 April. Deputy Chair Graham Oldroyd will revert to his former role as independent non-executive director. Chair Stephen Harris quote: “Given that we now have a stable platform for growth the board will be focusing on business opportunities and returning to a more common governance structure...We are actively pursuing the recruitment of a permanent chief executive officer and will update shareholders on progress with this search as soon as we have something able to.”

summary: Martin Cooke steps down as director; board restructures ahead of CEO search
body: Independent non-executive director Martin Cooke to step down 30 April. Deputy Chair Graham Oldroyd will revert to his former role as independent non-executive director. Chair Stephen Harris quote: “Given that we now have a stable platform for growth the board will be focusing on business opportunities and returning to a more common governance structure...We are actively pursuing the recruitment of a permanent chief executive officer and will update shareholders on progress with this search as soon as we have something able to.”

summary: Chair opposes board composition change, citing risk to commercialisation of company assets
body: Chair Jim Millen urges shareholders to vote against the resolution to change the board composition at the requisitioned GM (29 April). Chair Jim Millen quote: “The board rejects the suggestion that the company has failed to create value. In addition to delivering specialist services to clients, the company has built valuable intellectual property (Virtual Tumour and our personalised dosing tool which is in development), proprietary know-how, modelling capability, scientific credibility and customer relationships that represent important strategic assets of the business,” Millen adds there is “a genuine risk” that disruption to current stewardship without continuity of relevant company/sector knowledge could impair the ability to commercialise the value created.

summary: Chair opposes board composition changes, citing risk to commercialisation of company assets
body: Chair Jim Millen urges shareholders to vote against the resolution to change the board composition at the requisitioned GM (29 April). Chair Jim Millen quote: “The board rejects the suggestion that the company has failed to create value. In addition to delivering specialist services to clients, the company has built valuable intellectual property (Virtual Tumour and our personalised dosing tool which is in development), proprietary know-how, modelling capability, scientific credibility and customer relationships that represent important strategic assets of the business,” Millen adds there is “a genuine risk” that disruption to current stewardship without continuity of relevant company/sector knowledge could impair the ability to commercialise the value created.

summary: Victorian Plumbing launches four-year value creation plan tying executive pay to share price targets
body: Time Period Value creation plan with a four-year time horizon • Announces a value creation plan to “align executive leadership rewards directly with the delivery of sustained shareholder value”. • Updates the executive board’s long-term incentive arrangement via a one-off award “designed to incentivise the pursuit of strategic growth opportunities”. • Plan design links (and increases) executive compensation to share price milestone achievement. • Executive board “value creation pool” rises to GBP12.3 million if the share price reaches GBP2.75. • Executive board “value creation pool” rises to GBP20.5 million if the share price reaches GBP3.0. • The GBP20.5 million pool is GBP8.2 million higher than the GBP12.3 million pool (+66.7%). • CEO Stephnie Judge receives 25% of the overall incentive pool regardless of share price (implies GBP3.075 million at the GBP12.3m pool; GBP5.125 million at the GBP20.5m pool). • CFO Daniel Barton receives 20% of the overall incentive pool regardless of share price (implies GBP2.46 million at the GBP12.3m pool; GBP4.10 million at the GBP20.5m pool). • Implied remaining allocation to other executive board members is 55% of the overall pool (based on the stated CEO/CFO fixed percentages).

summary: Hunting PLC awarded $63.5 million titanium stress joint order for Guyana offshore development
body: Time Period Order delivery through May 2028; revenue recognition from H2 2026 • Awarded $63.5 million of orders for its titanium stress joint (TSJ) product line for a new offshore development in Guyana. • Award to be completed by the Group’s Subsea Spring business unit. • Deliveries scheduled through to May 2028. • Revenue expected to be recognised from H2 2026. • New TSJ order is in addition to $4.4 million of orders already secured for the same development since December 2025 (via Stafford and Flexible Engineered Solutions). • Total orders disclosed for this development since December 2025 are $67.9 million ($63.5m + $4.4m). • The $63.5m TSJ order is $59.1m higher than the $4.4m previously disclosed (+1,343%, or ~14.4x). • Company signals “further incremental orders likely to be received throughout the rest of 2026.” • Positions TSJ adoption on FPSO projects as supporting a strategy to capture more subsea spend by leveraging an enlarged product offering across the well life cycle. • CEO Jim Johnson said:“Our TSJ product line, which is a critical component for offshore developments, continues to be adopted on key offshore projects utilising FPSOs, given the maintenance benefits and reliability offered. “We would like to thank our partner in Guyana for its continued confidence in our product offering and look forward to working together in the future with our range of products and solutions. “This order also contributes to our guided subsea product group revenue and EBITDA through to 2028, as published at our subsea investor event held in January 2026.”

summary: Credit Acceptance Corp named 2026 USA Today Top Workplaces winner for sixth consecutive year
body: Time Period N/A (workplace award announcement) • Named a 2026 USA Today Top Workplaces award winner for the sixth consecutive year. • Ranked #4 in the 1,000–2,499 employee size category (Top 10 again this year). • Also recognised in the Top Workplaces “Well-Being” specialty category. • Quote (CEO Vinayak Hegde): “Being a Top Workplace reflects the commitment of our team members… and deliver on our mission of changing lives for dealers, consumers, and each other.” • Quote (CEO Vinayak Hegde) on culture/remote work: “In a remote work environment like ours, it’s especially important that we remain intentional about connecting and collaborating across teams and living our PRIDE values…” • Operational detail: nearly 95% of team is fully remote. • Connectivity initiatives cited: annual Sales Leadership Exchange, Support and Operations Alignment Retreat, plus in-office days and regional roundtable meetings. • “Second workplace award… this year”: earlier in the month, reached #18 on the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For® list (highest-ever ranking for the company). • Other accolades listed over the past year include (non-exhaustive): Fortune Best Workplace for Financial Services and Insurance™, Best Workplace for Women™, People Magazine’s Companies that Care®, Top Workplace for Remote Work, and Best Place to Work in IT (Computerworld). • Methodology: Top Workplaces awards based on an Energage employee survey measuring “Workplace Experience Themes” linked to high performance.

summary: Germany's Economy Ministry reviews BP's planned sale of Gelsenkirchen refinery to Klesch Group
body: Time Period Proposed refinery disposal (completion targeted H2 2026) + regulatory review Germany’s Economy Ministry is reviewing BP’s planned sale of its Gelsenkirchen refinery to Klesch Group (per Der Spiegel). Ministry spokesperson declined to comment, citing trade/business secrets. Germany may prohibit acquisitions by non-European investors under certain conditions (context for the review). BP previously announced it had struck an agreement for Klesch Group to acquire the refinery; completion targeted second half of 2026, subject to regulatory approval. Refinery detail: site annual processing capacity ~12 million tons of crude oil; primarily produces road and aviation transport fuels. Der Spiegel allegation: Klesch Group controlled via a network of subsidiaries registered in Jersey (described as a tax haven). Market reaction noted: BP shares +2.1% to 574.70p on the day mentioned, alongside higher Brent oil prices.

summary: Unilever to acquire Gruns Nutrition, a gummy supplements brand, strengthening its premium wellness portfolio
body: Time Period Announced acquisition agreement (closing expected later in 2026) Announced an agreement to acquire Gruns Nutrition Inc (gummy-form supplements), as part of portfolio shift “towards premium and high growth spaces”. Product scope: Gruns provides nutrient-support gummies for adults and children. Unilever’s positioning of target + origin story: “Founded in 2023 by Chad Janis, Gruns has a clear mission to make daily nutrient support an easy, enjoyable ritual. Janis launched the company after recognising that the supplement industry's biggest barrier wasn't awareness, but adherence. In a short time, Gruns has become one of the largest brands in the US Greens Supplement category, building a digitally native, culture-driven brand that resonates with consumers,” Unilever said. Strategic framing quote: “This marks another step in optimising Unilever's portfolio towards premium and high growth spaces, with a stronger focus on Beauty & Wellbeing and the US market.” Deal terms: not disclosed. Expected closing: later this year (2026), subject to regulatory approvals. Market reaction noted: Unilever shares -1.0% to 4,254.50p on the day mentioned. Note: this item appears twice in the provided pack (Articles 26 and 27) with the same content.

summary: TechForce Robotics partners with Oncotelic to develop GMP-compliant AI-enabled robotic platform
body: Time Period Broad industry commentary; references current regulatory shift + recent company initiatives Editorial frames EU GMP Annex 1 as updated guidance emphasising minimising human involvement in sterile production and encouraging barrier systems + automation to reduce contamination risk. Editorial claims FDA inspections continue to identify recurring compliance gaps (notably aseptic processing/documentation) and positions automation/data integrity as a growing compliance driver. Company-specific angle: TechForce Robotics described as building AI-enabled robotic platforms with SOP-based intelligence and real-time deviation detection aimed at regulated environments. Partnership noted: Nightfood/TechForce Robotics is said to be partnering with Oncotelic Therapeutics to advance commercialisation of a PDAOAI-enabled, GMP-compliant robotics platform. Quote included in the piece: “This milestone reflects the culmination of several years of research and development efforts, resulting in an integrated platform designed to combine Oncotelic's proprietary PDAOAI capabilities with TechForce's robotics hardware and manufacturing expertise. The system under development is designed to operate within GMP-regulated environments and is intended to enable automated material handling, real-time monitoring and PDAOAI-enhanced compliance workflows across pharmaceutical manufacturing and related applications.” Larger peer-name “context” included by the editorial (presented as examples of the trend, not company filings): mentions claimed initiatives involving NVIDIA, Johnson & Johnson, Amazon/AWS, and Tesla in physical AI/robotics-enabled workflows.

summary: Nightfood Holdings partners with Oncotelic on AI-enabled GMP-compliant robotic manufacturing platform
body: Time Period Industry / regulatory backdrop + company positioning update (no explicit period stated) • Editorial frames pharma manufacturing as undergoing “structural transformation” driven by tighter contamination control, data integrity and traceability expectations (EU GMP Annex 1; FDA inspection findings). • Core positioning claim: TechForce Robotics is developing AI-enabled robotic platforms combining autonomous functionality with SOP-based intelligence and real-time deviation detection, aimed at “continuous” compliance in GMP-regulated environments. • The piece argues existing automation “has not fully addressed” persistent compliance gaps (aseptic processing, documentation), implying a need for more context-aware / adaptive robotics vs “predefined task” robots. • States Nightfood Holdings (via TechForce Robotics) is partnering with Oncotelic Therapeutics on commercialising a “PDAOAI-enabled, GMP-compliant robotics platform.” • Full quote (collaboration milestone): “This milestone reflects the culmination of several years of research and development efforts, resulting in an integrated platform designed to combine Oncotelic's proprietary PDAOAI capabilities with TechForce's robotics hardware and manufacturing expertise,” the announcement stated. “The system under development is designed to operate within GMP-regulated environments and is intended to enable automated material handling, real-time monitoring and PDAOAI-enhanced compliance workflows across pharmaceutical manufacturing and related applications.” • Feed note: the same AINewsWire editorial appears multiple times in this file under different tickers (e.g., AMZN / NVDA contexts), but the profiled issuer content is essentially the same.

summary: Amazon Pharmacy launches Eli Lilly's GLP-1 pill Foundayo with same-day delivery expansion
body: Time Period Product availability launch + delivery expansion (current; expansion “by year end”) • Amazon Pharmacy now offers Eli Lilly’s GLP-1 pill Foundayo™ (once-daily oral for adults with obesity or overweight with weight-related medical problems). • Pricing disclosed: with insurance “as low as $1 per day / $25 per month”; cash pay “$5 per day / $149 per month,” with manufacturer-sponsored coupons applied automatically pre-checkout. • Delivery footprint: Same-Day Delivery in “nearly 3,000 cities and towns,” expanding to “nearly 4,500 by year end” (implies +~1,500 locations / ~50% increase). • Service proposition vs mail-order: in remote areas, Amazon cites next-/two-/three-day options versus “five to 10 days” for other mail-order delivery services. • New channel: plans “soon” for in-office, licensed pharmacist-supported kiosks at select One Medical sites, enabling pickup “within minutes” after appointments. • Amazon Pharmacy quote (Tanvi Patel): “We are giving customers faster treatment options with our Same-Day Delivery offering, easy ways to save with automatically applied manufacturer coupons and the price transparency they need to make informed decisions about their care.” • Lilly quote (Laura Steele): “If prescribed, patients may be able to have Foundayo on their doorstep the same day or walk out of their doctor's office with it in-hand in Los Angeles — eliminating the waiting and uncertainty that patients may face when starting their treatment journey.” • Additional metrics claimed: Amazon Pharmacy says customers have saved “more than $200 million” through automatic coupons, with GLP-1 medications the largest savings category; it also notes GLP-1 delivery since 2021 and an existing collaboration with LillyDirect for insured customers.

summary: Citi forecasts 9% non-NII growth for Lloyds in 2026 despite UK macro concerns
body:
• Non-NII: Citi forecasts +9% growth in non-net interest income in 2026.
  • Citi says the principal investor pushback is macro/political (UK outlook) rather than company-specific.
  • Political comments included: poor Labour local election showing “widely anticipated”; leadership change “remains unlikely”; UK fiscal position “better than feared” (better positioned for an energy shock).
  • Citi also notes: loan demand remains strong (esp. large corporates); unemployment rising but mainly among under-24s (small share of bank lending).
  • Market data included in note: current stock price 99.93p; 12-month change +55%.
  • Article appears twice in the pack (Articles 37 and 38) with the same content.

summary: BP discovers 2 trillion cubic feet gas offshore Egypt with partner Eni
body: Time Period New offshore Egypt gas discovery (reported “on Wednesday” with market move “on Thursday morning”) • BP reported a new gas deposit offshore Egypt (shared interest with Eni). • Discovery well: Denise W-1, part of the Temsah concession (Eastern Mediterranean). • Eni preliminary estimates: ~2 trillion cubic feet gas initially in place + 130 million barrels associated condensates. • Location: ~70km offshore, 95m water depth; <10km from existing infrastructure. • Reservoir quality/thickness: “High-quality gas-bearing sandstone reservoirs” across a ~90m gross interval. • BP positioning: says it’s BP’s second exploration discovery this year, following 12 discoveries in 2025. • Temsah concession: BP previously entered a binding agreement (July) with Egyptian state entities for a 20-year renewal. • Working interest: BP 50% and Eni 50%; Eni operates the Denise development lease via Petrobel (Eni/EGPC JV). • Other Egypt positions mentioned: BP took 100% of North-East El Alamein offshore concession and 25% of West El Hammad; also signed for Red Sea Block 6. • Market/context datapoints included: Brent referenced at $97.40/bbl vs $95.20 prior close; BP shares +2.3% to 575.78p (also +13% past month; +73% past year). • Corporate note included: appointment of new BP CEO Meg O’Neill and Deputy CEO Carol Howle referenced as occurring “last week”.

summary: ME Group partners with Asda to deploy up to 700 laundry machines across UK stores
body: Time Period Partnership announcement; rollout target stated for financial 2026 • New partnership with Asda Stores Ltd to install/operate laundry machines across Asda Supercentre, Superstore, supermarket and petrol forecourt sites in the UK. • Scale target: up to 700 laundry machines across these Asda locations (described as high-footfall). • Strategic linkage: ME says this supports its growth plan to install 1,300 laundry machines in financial 2026. • Long-term ambition stated: install >20,000 laundry machines globally. • Current footprint stated: >7,600 machines across 12 countries; >1,500 in the UK & Republic of Ireland. • Deputy CEO Vladimir Crasneanscki quote: “Asda is a perfect company to partner with, their retail and petrol stations are excellent locations for our services and their innovative and fast-paced approach will enable us to deploy our units rapidly.” • Deputy CEO Vladimir Crasneanscki quote: “We are delighted to announce this partnership; this is the largest single client deal in the history of our laundry division and demonstrates the growing demand for our services.” • Share price reaction in the piece: +3.7% to 147.00p (Thursday morning).

summary: Great Portland Estates fully lets SIX St Andrew Street at above valuation with strong rental yield
body: Time Period Completion of a leasing campaign (at SIX St Andrew Street, Farringdon) • Let the final remaining office space at SIX St Andrew Street (Farringdon, London) — 3rd and 4th floors, totaling >11,680 sq ft — to an AI company. • Building now fully occupied and generating >£8.8m annual rent at an average of £200/sq ft. • Letting metrics: rent level stated as 6.2% ahead of ERV and 6.2% yield on cost. • Portfolio mix datapoint: AI-related customers represent 11.7% of total office portfolio, or 25.2% of fully managed offices. • GPE quote: “This letting reflects the strong momentum in GPE's fully managed portfolio, where leasing activity accelerated during the final quarter of the financial year… with 19 deals completed and a further seven currently under offer.” • Molly Maguire (Senior Flex Leasing manager) quote: “Completing the leasing campaign at SIX marks a great milestone for our Fully Managed portfolio. Demand continues to be driven by customers looking for high quality, flexible workspace in well-connected central locations, and SIX has proven to be an excellent example of how that proposition resonates.” • Share price move in the piece: -0.9% to 305.80p (Thursday morning).

summary: LGI Homes launches Topaz at Skye Canyon, a 49-home townhome community in Las Vegas starting at $380s
body: Time Period N/A (community launch announcement) • Announced grand opening of Topaz at Skye Canyon, a new townhome community within the Skye Canyon development in Las Vegas. • Community size: 49 homesites. • Pricing: homes start from the “$380s.” • Product range: 3- and 4-bedroom townhome floor plans with attached two-car garages. • Four floor plans disclosed with sizes: 1,256 / 1,432 / 1,533 / 1,665 sq ft. • Includes “CompleteHome Plus™” upgrade package (e.g., energy-efficient Whirlpool stainless steel appliances, 42” wood cabinetry, tile backsplash, designer finishes). • Quote (Ron Christian, VP Operations – Nevada): “Expanding into Skye Canyon is an exciting opportunity for LGI Homes… It’s the perfect combination of new construction and a proven community setting where homeowners can feel at home from the very first day.”

summary: Blackbird integrates Epidemic Sound's royalty-free music library directly into its video editor platform
body: Time Period Current product launch (no financial period specified). Launched integrated royalty-free music from Epidemic Sound inside (Blackbird’s browser-based collaborative video editor). All users can now access Epidemic Sound’s catalogue directly within the editor (search/preview/add tracks without leaving the workflow). Licensing is handled at export, and can be tailored to user requirements. Chair Ian McDonough quote: “Music is fundamental to great storytelling, it's what gives video content its emotion and energy. So rather than sending creators off to find tracks elsewhere, we've built it straight into the platform. World-class music, right there in users' workflows. No interruptions, just creativity and complete peace of mind. This is a huge moment for the company and we're incredibly excited.”

summary: JV partner to drill 1,500 metres at Elizabeth Hill seeking deeper high-grade silver extensions
body: Time Period Drilling early April 2026; assay results expected progressively through Q2 2026. JV partner West Coast Silver Ltd plans to start a 1,500-metre diamond drilling programme in early April at Elizabeth Hill Silver Project (near Karratha, Pilbara, Western Australia). Drill programme expected duration: 4–6 weeks. Assay results expected progressively throughout Q2. Drilling focus: test for extensions of mineralisation along strike of the historic Elizabeth Hill high-grade underground silver mine, plus structural targets along the Munni Munni Fault. Chair Bruce Garlick quote: “Our previous 2025 drilling confirmed that silver mineralisation at Elizabeth Hill extends beyond the historical underground mine and associated with a broader structural corridor along the Munni Munni Fault. This current program of diamond drilling below Elizabeth Hill is designed to determine whether there are high-grade silver extensions below and south of the mine. The drilling will also test whether high-grade mineralisation repeats at depth; a model that, if confirmed, could materially expand the resource potential.”

company: GreenTech Metals Ltd (JV partner update relevant to Alien Metals’ Munni Munni exposure)
summary: GreenTech identifies material upside at Munni Munni beyond historic resource estimate
body: Time Period Current exploration update (no financial period specified). GreenTech reports “material upside potential” at Munni Munni Platinum-Palladium-Copper-Nickel Project not captured in the historic mineral resource estimate. Historic MRE cited: 24 million tonnes @ 2.9 g/t (Pt, Pd, Rh & Au) for 2.2 million ounces total. Upside items highlighted: high-grade PGE zones above 4 g/t. Upside items highlighted: under-recognised copper and nickel mineralisation outside reef domains. Upside items highlighted: near-surface eastern mineralisation that may be suitable for open-pit mining.

summary: IG Group plans to expand mineral processing hubs across Africa and Latin America
body: Time Period Goals for the next 12 months. Targets launch of multiple processing hubs across Tanzania, Africa, and Latin America. Plans to scale mineral aggregation network via new artisanal & small-scale miners and semi-industrial partnerships. Intends to secure long-term agreements aligned with EU and US critical mineral requirements. Plans to progress ventures on high-scale assets confirmed through rapid validation protocols. Company comment quote: “The synergy between Amigo's visionary leadership and aggressive operational execution has created a platform of Tier-One potential. By controlling the processing nodes and the information layer of the African mineral economy, we are delivering a new standard of transparency and growth. We stand at a pivotal inflection point, uniquely positioned to capture the multi-billion-dollar industrial upside of African mineral beneficiation.”

summary: Crystal Amber Fund restarts share buyback of 2.7 million shares for £3.0 million
body: Time Period Buyback restart (no financial period specified). Restarted share buyback programme. Intends to repurchase 2.7 million shares worth GBP3.0 million under the authorisation granted on 2 December. Notes there is no guarantee the programme will be implemented in full.

summary: Eden Research PLC's February placing conditions satisfied following EIS/VCT legislative changes
body: Time Period Placing conditions now satisfied (placing announced in February). Confirms all conditions relating to its conditional placing are now satisfied. February placing raised GBP7.6 million gross at 4.0p per share. Conditionality referenced: in part dependent on EIS/VCT legislative changes (announced in the UK Government Autumn Statement) passing into law.

summary: Quantum Helium begins extended production test at Sagebrush-1 well in Colorado
body: Time Period Extended production test to run “over the coming weeks”. Began an extended production test at Sagebrush-1 well (Colorado). Testing expected to run over the coming weeks; results to be announced after analysis is completed. CEO Howard McLaughlin quote: “Commencing the extended production test at Sagebrush-1 is a major milestone for Quantum and an important step forward for the company. This well has already demonstrated the presence of helium, and this programme is focused on confirming flow rates and commercial potential. The results will be critical in moving our prospective resources towards reserves and defining the pathway to development.”

summary: Guardian Metal Resources received notice of 300,000 share option exercises raising GBP42,000
body: Time Period Option exercise notice (no financial period specified). Received notice of the exercise of options over 300,000 shares, raising GBP42,000. Exercise price stated as 0.14p per share; note this appears arithmetically inconsistent with GBP42,000 proceeds (GBP42,000 over 300,000 shares implies 14p per share).

summary: Tullow Oil obtained required consents to modify USD1.29bn notes due 2026
body: Time Period Consent solicitation for notes due 2026 (market data referenced “Wednesday afternoon”). Received requisite consents in a consent solicitation related to USD1.29bn 10.25% senior secured notes due 2026. Brent oil referenced at USD95.20/bbl (Wednesday afternoon) vs USD110.24/bbl around the same time the prior day (-13.6% day-on-day). Current stock price referenced: 12.46p, -3.7% on Wednesday; 12-month -8.9%.

summary: Jubilee Metals shareholders approved capital reduction to boost distributable reserves for future dividends
body: Time Period General Meeting outcome (reported “on Wednesday”). Shareholders approved proposed capital reduction plan. Rationale stated: restructure balance sheet to increase distributable reserves to support potential future dividends and share buybacks. Notes that a resolution to grant directors authority to allot shares was withdrawn. Share prices referenced: Johannesburg 74c (+4.2%) with 12-month -1.3%; London 3.23p (+1.1%) with 12-month +14%.

summary: Rebecca Papp sold 4,033 Next shares worth £521,265 at £129.25 each
body: Time Period Director/PCA dealing (announcement day) • Rebecca Papp (person closely associated with Group Merchandise & Operations Director Richard Papp) sold 4,033 shares at £129.25 each. • Total value of sale: £521,265. • “Current share price” cited: 13,305p (≈ £133.05), implying the cited market price is ~+2.9% above the disclosed sale price. • “12-month change” cited: +21%.

summary: Shell's Q1 2026 output guidance cut due to Middle East conflict impact on Qatar operations
body: Time Period Q1 2026 trading update (guidance ranges) • Integrated Gas output expected 880k–920k boepd in Q1, down from 948k boepd in Q4 2025 (midpoint implies ~-5.1% QoQ). • Integrated Gas guidance reduced vs prior range 920k–980k boepd (new lower end -40k / ~-4.3% vs old lower end; new upper end -60k / ~-6.1% vs old upper end). • Shell attributes Integrated Gas weakness to “the impact of the Middle East conflict on Qatari volumes” and flags: “in light of the ongoing situation in the Middle East, the outlook provided is subject to increased uncertainty.” • LNG volume guidance nudged up to 7.6–8.0mt from 7.4–8.0mt (lower end +0.2mt / ~+2.7%); Q4 LNG volumes were 7.8mt. • LNG drivers: “ramp-up of LNG Canada” offset by “Australia weather constraints” and “Qatar LNG outages.” • Upstream production expected 1.76–1.86m boepd, down from 1.89m boepd in Q4 (midpoint implies ~-4.2% QoQ); includes reduced production after the Adura JV incorporation. • Chemicals & Products indicative refining margin: $17/bbl vs $14/bbl in Q4 (+21.4% QoQ). • Trading statement language: “trading & optimisation is expected to be significantly higher” than Q4. • Balance sheet: non-cash net debt expected to be impacted by a $3bn–$4bn increase in “variable components of long-term shipping leases.” • Working capital expected to “swing” to negative $10m–$15m in Q1 vs positive $1.3bn in the prior quarter (as stated in the article). • Market/analyst colour included in the piece: AJ Bell describes a “dichotomy” where higher energy prices help profits but Middle East footprint disrupts operations; JPMorgan flags a pricing vs balance sheet “timing mis-match” and a “heavy… working capital build.” • Share price / macro context cited: Shell shares -5.3% to 3,377.70p; Brent crude cited at $93.76/bbl vs $110.24 at prior London close (about -15.0%) after the US and Iran agreed a two-week ceasefire.

summary: Close Brothers estimates £320m FCA motor finance redress cost, manageable within capital resources
body: Time Period FCA motor finance redress scheme update (current) • FCA scheme scope cited: 12.1m agreements eligible vs 14.2m at consultation stage (reduction of 2.1m / ~-14.8%). • FCA scheme cost cited: ~£7.5bn compensation; ~£9.1bn total incl. admin (vs earlier projections up to £11bn). • Close Brothers estimates final scheme cost at around £320m, described as “broadly similar” to existing IAS 37 provision of £294m (delta +£26m / ~+8.8%). • Close Brothers language: cost can be “comfortably absorbed” by existing capital resources, leaving it “well positioned” to keep “delivering its strategy.” • Provisioning status: “At this stage, no provision changes have been recognised. The group's existing IAS 37 provision remains under review,” and it will monitor further legal/regulatory/industry developments and consider next steps. • Capital impact: estimate would trim CET1 by ~25bps to 14.0% pro forma; remains above medium-term target 12%–13%. • UBS takeaways in the piece: outcome “reduces potential downside risks”; highlights that FirstRand increased provisions and that Viceroy Research argued Close Brothers would need to at least double provisions. • UBS sensitivity detail (as reported): the £320m estimate assumes 75% scheme take-up; each 5% change = £18m impact and 17bps CET1 impact. • Panmure Liberum scenario (as reported): assuming 100% take-up and excluding a £16m time value discount implies total cost around £426m, and the broker still expects sufficient CET1 headroom. • Shore Capital action (as reported): cut to ‘hold’ with “Take profits,” noting shares up 33% since its upgrade; warns “underlying operational performance remains weak” and “significant execution will be required.” • Share moves cited across the two wires: +16% to 451.54p in the longer “EXTRA” story; +18% to 457.94p in the shorter item.

summary: Greatland Resources Q3 gold production of 82,723oz boosted cash to $1.21bn with no debt
body: Time Period Quarter ended March 2026 (FY Q3) + YTD FY2026 • Q3 production: 82,723 oz gold and 4,128 tonnes copper. • YTD production: 249,887 oz gold and 11.022 tonnes copper (as stated). • Q3 sales exceeded production: sold 97,800 oz gold and 4,620 tonnes copper (vs produced 82,723 oz / 4,128t). • YTD sales: 252,212 oz gold and 10.333 tonnes copper (as stated). • Cash build: “approximately $260m” in the quarter after capex and a $73m tax payment. • Balance sheet: $1.21bn cash and no debt at 31 March, up from $948m at end-2025 (increase $262m / ~+27.6%). • Guidance: now expects full-year production “around or slightly above” the upper end of prior guidance 260k–310k oz gold. • Operational note: Telfer not impacted by diesel supply disruptions linked to Middle East war because plant largely powered by onsite natural-gas generation; diesel used only for highest-grade ore sources. • Hedging note: retains full upside exposure to gold price with partial downside protection from put options. • Share move cited: +14% to 762.50p.

summary: Rebecca Papp sells 4,033 Next shares worth £521,265 at £129.25 each
body: Time Period Director/PCA dealing (announcement day) • Rebecca Papp (person closely associated with Group Merchandise & Operations Director Richard Papp) sold 4,033 shares at £129.25 each. • Total value of sale: £521,265. • “Current share price” cited: 13,305p (≈ £133.05), implying the cited market price is ~+2.9% above the disclosed sale price. • “12-month change” cited: +21%.

summary: Shell Q1 2026 guidance cut due to Middle East conflict impact on Qatar LNG production
body: Time Period Q1 2026 trading update (guidance ranges) • Integrated Gas output expected 880k–920k boepd in Q1, down from 948k boepd in Q4 2025 (midpoint implies ~-5.1% QoQ). • Integrated Gas guidance reduced vs prior range 920k–980k boepd (new lower end -40k / ~-4.3% vs old lower end; new upper end -60k / ~-6.1% vs old upper end). • Shell attributes Integrated Gas weakness to “the impact of the Middle East conflict on Qatari volumes” and flags: “in light of the ongoing situation in the Middle East, the outlook provided is subject to increased uncertainty.” • LNG volume guidance nudged up to 7.6–8.0mt from 7.4–8.0mt (lower end +0.2mt / ~+2.7%); Q4 LNG volumes were 7.8mt. • LNG drivers: “ramp-up of LNG Canada” offset by “Australia weather constraints” and “Qatar LNG outages.” • Upstream production expected 1.76–1.86m boepd, down from 1.89m boepd in Q4 (midpoint implies ~-4.2% QoQ); includes reduced production after the Adura JV incorporation. • Chemicals & Products indicative refining margin: $17/bbl vs $14/bbl in Q4 (+21.4% QoQ). • Trading statement language: “trading & optimisation is expected to be significantly higher” than Q4. • Balance sheet: non-cash net debt expected to be impacted by a $3bn–$4bn increase in “variable components of long-term shipping leases.” • Working capital expected to “swing” to negative $10m–$15m in Q1 vs positive $1.3bn in the prior quarter (as stated in the article). • Market/analyst colour included in the piece: AJ Bell describes a “dichotomy” where higher energy prices help profits but Middle East footprint disrupts operations; JPMorgan flags a pricing vs balance sheet “timing mis-match” and a “heavy… working capital build.” • Share price / macro context cited: Shell shares -5.3% to 3,377.70p; Brent crude cited at $93.76/bbl vs $110.24 at prior London close (about -15.0%) after the US and Iran agreed a two-week ceasefire.

summary: Close Brothers estimates £320m FCA motor finance redress cost, below earlier £11bn sector projection
body: Time Period FCA motor finance redress scheme update (current) • FCA scheme scope cited: 12.1m agreements eligible vs 14.2m at consultation stage (reduction of 2.1m / ~-14.8%). • FCA scheme cost cited: ~£7.5bn compensation; ~£9.1bn total incl. admin (vs earlier projections up to £11bn). • Close Brothers estimates final scheme cost at around £320m, described as “broadly similar” to existing IAS 37 provision of £294m (delta +£26m / ~+8.8%). • Close Brothers language: cost can be “comfortably absorbed” by existing capital resources, leaving it “well positioned” to keep “delivering its strategy.” • Provisioning status: “At this stage, no provision changes have been recognised. The group's existing IAS 37 provision remains under review,” and it will monitor further legal/regulatory/industry developments and consider next steps. • Capital impact: estimate would trim CET1 by ~25bps to 14.0% pro forma; remains above medium-term target 12%–13%. • UBS takeaways in the piece: outcome “reduces potential downside risks”; highlights that FirstRand increased provisions and that Viceroy Research argued Close Brothers would need to at least double provisions. • UBS sensitivity detail (as reported): the £320m estimate assumes 75% scheme take-up; each 5% change = £18m impact and 17bps CET1 impact. • Panmure Liberum scenario (as reported): assuming 100% take-up and excluding a £16m time value discount implies total cost around £426m, and the broker still expects sufficient CET1 headroom. • Shore Capital action (as reported): cut to ‘hold’ with “Take profits,” noting shares up 33% since its upgrade; warns “underlying operational performance remains weak” and “significant execution will be required.” • Share moves cited across the two wires: +16% to 451.54p in the longer “EXTRA” story; +18% to 457.94p in the shorter item.

summary: Greatland Resources posts strong Q3 gold production with $1.21bn cash and raised FY guidance
body: Time Period Quarter ended March 2026 (FY Q3) + YTD FY2026 • Q3 production: 82,723 oz gold and 4,128 tonnes copper. • YTD production: 249,887 oz gold and 11.022 tonnes copper (as stated). • Q3 sales exceeded production: sold 97,800 oz gold and 4,620 tonnes copper (vs produced 82,723 oz / 4,128t). • YTD sales: 252,212 oz gold and 10.333 tonnes copper (as stated). • Cash build: “approximately $260m” in the quarter after capex and a $73m tax payment. • Balance sheet: $1.21bn cash and no debt at 31 March, up from $948m at end-2025 (increase $262m / ~+27.6%). • Guidance: now expects full-year production “around or slightly above” the upper end of prior guidance 260k–310k oz gold. • Operational note: Telfer not impacted by diesel supply disruptions linked to Middle East war because plant largely powered by onsite natural-gas generation; diesel used only for highest-grade ore sources. • Hedging note: retains full upside exposure to gold price with partial downside protection from put options. • Share move cited: +14% to 762.50p.

summary: Victorian Plumbing launches four-year value creation plan tying executive pay to share price milestones
body: Time Period Value creation plan with a four-year time horizon • Announces a value creation plan to “align executive leadership rewards directly with the delivery of sustained shareholder value”. • Updates the executive board’s long-term incentive arrangement via a one-off award “designed to incentivise the pursuit of strategic growth opportunities”. • Plan design links (and increases) executive compensation to share price milestone achievement. • Executive board “value creation pool” rises to GBP12.3 million if the share price reaches GBP2.75. • Executive board “value creation pool” rises to GBP20.5 million if the share price reaches GBP3.0. • The GBP20.5 million pool is GBP8.2 million higher than the GBP12.3 million pool (+66.7%). • CEO Stephnie Judge receives 25% of the overall incentive pool regardless of share price (implies GBP3.075 million at the GBP12.3m pool; GBP5.125 million at the GBP20.5m pool). • CFO Daniel Barton receives 20% of the overall incentive pool regardless of share price (implies GBP2.46 million at the GBP12.3m pool; GBP4.10 million at the GBP20.5m pool). • Implied remaining allocation to other executive board members is 55% of the overall pool (based on the stated CEO/CFO fixed percentages).

summary: Hunting wins $63.5m titanium stress joint order for Guyana offshore development through 2028
body: Time Period Order delivery through May 2028; revenue recognition from H2 2026 • Awarded $63.5 million of orders for its titanium stress joint (TSJ) product line for a new offshore development in Guyana. • Award to be completed by the Group’s Subsea Spring business unit. • Deliveries scheduled through to May 2028. • Revenue expected to be recognised from H2 2026. • New TSJ order is in addition to $4.4 million of orders already secured for the same development since December 2025 (via Stafford and Flexible Engineered Solutions). • Total orders disclosed for this development since December 2025 are $67.9 million ($63.5m + $4.4m). • The $63.5m TSJ order is $59.1m higher than the $4.4m previously disclosed (+1,343%, or ~14.4x). • Company signals “further incremental orders likely to be received throughout the rest of 2026.” • Positions TSJ adoption on FPSO projects as supporting a strategy to capture more subsea spend by leveraging an enlarged product offering across the well life cycle. • CEO Jim Johnson said:“Our TSJ product line, which is a critical component for offshore developments, continues to be adopted on key offshore projects utilising FPSOs, given the maintenance benefits and reliability offered. “We would like to thank our partner in Guyana for its continued confidence in our product offering and look forward to working together in the future with our range of products and solutions. “This order also contributes to our guided subsea product group revenue and EBITDA through to 2028, as published at our subsea investor event held in January 2026.”

summary: Credit Acceptance named 2026 USA Today Top Workplaces winner for sixth consecutive year
body: Time Period N/A (workplace award announcement) • Named a 2026 USA Today Top Workplaces award winner for the sixth consecutive year. • Ranked #4 in the 1,000–2,499 employee size category (Top 10 again this year). • Also recognised in the Top Workplaces “Well-Being” specialty category. • Quote (CEO Vinayak Hegde): “Being a Top Workplace reflects the commitment of our team members… and deliver on our mission of changing lives for dealers, consumers, and each other.” • Quote (CEO Vinayak Hegde) on culture/remote work: “In a remote work environment like ours, it’s especially important that we remain intentional about connecting and collaborating across teams and living our PRIDE values…” • Operational detail: nearly 95% of team is fully remote. • Connectivity initiatives cited: annual Sales Leadership Exchange, Support and Operations Alignment Retreat, plus in-office days and regional roundtable meetings. • “Second workplace award… this year”: earlier in the month, reached #18 on the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For® list (highest-ever ranking for the company). • Other accolades listed over the past year include (non-exhaustive): Fortune Best Workplace for Financial Services and Insurance™, Best Workplace for Women™, People Magazine’s Companies that Care®, Top Workplace for Remote Work, and Best Place to Work in IT (Computerworld). • Methodology: Top Workplaces awards based on an Energage employee survey measuring “Workplace Experience Themes” linked to high performance.

summary: Germany reviews BP's planned sale of Gelsenkirchen refinery to Klesch Group
body: Time Period Proposed refinery disposal (completion targeted H2 2026) + regulatory review Germany’s Economy Ministry is reviewing BP’s planned sale of its Gelsenkirchen refinery to Klesch Group (per Der Spiegel). Ministry spokesperson declined to comment, citing trade/business secrets. Germany may prohibit acquisitions by non-European investors under certain conditions (context for the review). BP previously announced it had struck an agreement for Klesch Group to acquire the refinery; completion targeted second half of 2026, subject to regulatory approval. Refinery detail: site annual processing capacity ~12 million tons of crude oil; primarily produces road and aviation transport fuels. Der Spiegel allegation: Klesch Group controlled via a network of subsidiaries registered in Jersey (described as a tax haven). Market reaction noted: BP shares +2.1% to 574.70p on the day mentioned, alongside higher Brent oil prices.

summary: Unilever to acquire gummy supplements brand Gruns in 2026 portfolio shift
body: Time Period Announced acquisition agreement (closing expected later in 2026) Announced an agreement to acquire Gruns Nutrition Inc (gummy-form supplements), as part of portfolio shift “towards premium and high growth spaces”. Product scope: Gruns provides nutrient-support gummies for adults and children. Unilever’s positioning of target + origin story: “Founded in 2023 by Chad Janis, Gruns has a clear mission to make daily nutrient support an easy, enjoyable ritual. Janis launched the company after recognising that the supplement industry's biggest barrier wasn't awareness, but adherence. In a short time, Gruns has become one of the largest brands in the US Greens Supplement category, building a digitally native, culture-driven brand that resonates with consumers,” Unilever said. Strategic framing quote: “This marks another step in optimising Unilever's portfolio towards premium and high growth spaces, with a stronger focus on Beauty & Wellbeing and the US market.” Deal terms: not disclosed. Expected closing: later this year (2026), subject to regulatory approvals. Market reaction noted: Unilever shares -1.0% to 4,254.50p on the day mentioned. Note: this item appears twice in the provided pack (Articles 26 and 27) with the same content.

summary: Nightfood and TechForce Robotics partner with Oncotelic to develop GMP-compliant AI-enabled pharmaceutical manufacturing platform
body: Time Period Broad industry commentary; references current regulatory shift + recent company initiatives Editorial frames EU GMP Annex 1 as updated guidance emphasising minimising human involvement in sterile production and encouraging barrier systems + automation to reduce contamination risk. Editorial claims FDA inspections continue to identify recurring compliance gaps (notably aseptic processing/documentation) and positions automation/data integrity as a growing compliance driver. Company-specific angle: TechForce Robotics described as building AI-enabled robotic platforms with SOP-based intelligence and real-time deviation detection aimed at regulated environments. Partnership noted: Nightfood/TechForce Robotics is said to be partnering with Oncotelic Therapeutics to advance commercialisation of a PDAOAI-enabled, GMP-compliant robotics platform. Quote included in the piece: “This milestone reflects the culmination of several years of research and development efforts, resulting in an integrated platform designed to combine Oncotelic's proprietary PDAOAI capabilities with TechForce's robotics hardware and manufacturing expertise. The system under development is designed to operate within GMP-regulated environments and is intended to enable automated material handling, real-time monitoring and PDAOAI-enhanced compliance workflows across pharmaceutical manufacturing and related applications.” Larger peer-name “context” included by the editorial (presented as examples of the trend, not company filings): mentions claimed initiatives involving NVIDIA, Johnson & Johnson, Amazon/AWS, and Tesla in physical AI/robotics-enabled workflows.

summary: TechForce Robotics partners with Oncotelic on AI-enabled GMP-compliant pharmaceutical manufacturing automation platform
body: Time Period Industry / regulatory backdrop + company positioning update (no explicit period stated) • Editorial frames pharma manufacturing as undergoing “structural transformation” driven by tighter contamination control, data integrity and traceability expectations (EU GMP Annex 1; FDA inspection findings). • Core positioning claim: TechForce Robotics is developing AI-enabled robotic platforms combining autonomous functionality with SOP-based intelligence and real-time deviation detection, aimed at “continuous” compliance in GMP-regulated environments. • The piece argues existing automation “has not fully addressed” persistent compliance gaps (aseptic processing, documentation), implying a need for more context-aware / adaptive robotics vs “predefined task” robots. • States Nightfood Holdings (via TechForce Robotics) is partnering with Oncotelic Therapeutics on commercialising a “PDAOAI-enabled, GMP-compliant robotics platform.” • Full quote (collaboration milestone): “This milestone reflects the culmination of several years of research and development efforts, resulting in an integrated platform designed to combine Oncotelic's proprietary PDAOAI capabilities with TechForce's robotics hardware and manufacturing expertise,” the announcement stated. “The system under development is designed to operate within GMP-regulated environments and is intended to enable automated material handling, real-time monitoring and PDAOAI-enhanced compliance workflows across pharmaceutical manufacturing and related applications.” • Feed note: the same AINewsWire editorial appears multiple times in this file under different tickers (e.g., AMZN / NVDA contexts), but the profiled issuer content is essentially the same.

summary: Amazon Pharmacy launches Eli Lilly's GLP-1 pill Foundayo with same-day delivery nationwide
body: Time Period Product availability launch + delivery expansion (current; expansion “by year end”) • Amazon Pharmacy now offers Eli Lilly’s GLP-1 pill Foundayo™ (once-daily oral for adults with obesity or overweight with weight-related medical problems). • Pricing disclosed: with insurance “as low as $1 per day / $25 per month”; cash pay “$5 per day / $149 per month,” with manufacturer-sponsored coupons applied automatically pre-checkout. • Delivery footprint: Same-Day Delivery in “nearly 3,000 cities and towns,” expanding to “nearly 4,500 by year end” (implies +~1,500 locations / ~50% increase). • Service proposition vs mail-order: in remote areas, Amazon cites next-/two-/three-day options versus “five to 10 days” for other mail-order delivery services. • New channel: plans “soon” for in-office, licensed pharmacist-supported kiosks at select One Medical sites, enabling pickup “within minutes” after appointments. • Amazon Pharmacy quote (Tanvi Patel): “We are giving customers faster treatment options with our Same-Day Delivery offering, easy ways to save with automatically applied manufacturer coupons and the price transparency they need to make informed decisions about their care.” • Lilly quote (Laura Steele): “If prescribed, patients may be able to have Foundayo on their doorstep the same day or walk out of their doctor's office with it in-hand in Los Angeles — eliminating the waiting and uncertainty that patients may face when starting their treatment journey.” • Additional metrics claimed: Amazon Pharmacy says customers have saved “more than $200 million” through automatic coupons, with GLP-1 medications the largest savings category; it also notes GLP-1 delivery since 2021 and an existing collaboration with LillyDirect for insured customers.

summary: Citi forecasts 9% non-NII growth for Lloyds in 2026 amid macro concerns
body:
• Non-NII: Citi forecasts +9% growth in non-net interest income in 2026.
  • Citi says the principal investor pushback is macro/political (UK outlook) rather than company-specific.
  • Political comments included: poor Labour local election showing “widely anticipated”; leadership change “remains unlikely”; UK fiscal position “better than feared” (better positioned for an energy shock).
  • Citi also notes: loan demand remains strong (esp. large corporates); unemployment rising but mainly among under-24s (small share of bank lending).
  • Market data included in note: current stock price 99.93p; 12-month change +55%.
  • Article appears twice in the pack (Articles 37 and 38) with the same content.

summary: BP discovers major gas deposit offshore Egypt with partner Eni in Eastern Mediterranean
body: Time Period New offshore Egypt gas discovery (reported “on Wednesday” with market move “on Thursday morning”) • BP reported a new gas deposit offshore Egypt (shared interest with Eni). • Discovery well: Denise W-1, part of the Temsah concession (Eastern Mediterranean). • Eni preliminary estimates: ~2 trillion cubic feet gas initially in place + 130 million barrels associated condensates. • Location: ~70km offshore, 95m water depth; <10km from existing infrastructure. • Reservoir quality/thickness: “High-quality gas-bearing sandstone reservoirs” across a ~90m gross interval. • BP positioning: says it’s BP’s second exploration discovery this year, following 12 discoveries in 2025. • Temsah concession: BP previously entered a binding agreement (July) with Egyptian state entities for a 20-year renewal. • Working interest: BP 50% and Eni 50%; Eni operates the Denise development lease via Petrobel (Eni/EGPC JV). • Other Egypt positions mentioned: BP took 100% of North-East El Alamein offshore concession and 25% of West El Hammad; also signed for Red Sea Block 6. • Market/context datapoints included: Brent referenced at $97.40/bbl vs $95.20 prior close; BP shares +2.3% to 575.78p (also +13% past month; +73% past year). • Corporate note included: appointment of new BP CEO Meg O’Neill and Deputy CEO Carol Howle referenced as occurring “last week”.

summary: ME Group announces landmark Asda partnership to deploy up to 700 laundry machines
body: Time Period Partnership announcement; rollout target stated for financial 2026 • New partnership with Asda Stores Ltd to install/operate laundry machines across Asda Supercentre, Superstore, supermarket and petrol forecourt sites in the UK. • Scale target: up to 700 laundry machines across these Asda locations (described as high-footfall). • Strategic linkage: ME says this supports its growth plan to install 1,300 laundry machines in financial 2026. • Long-term ambition stated: install >20,000 laundry machines globally. • Current footprint stated: >7,600 machines across 12 countries; >1,500 in the UK & Republic of Ireland. • Deputy CEO Vladimir Crasneanscki quote: “Asda is a perfect company to partner with, their retail and petrol stations are excellent locations for our services and their innovative and fast-paced approach will enable us to deploy our units rapidly.” • Deputy CEO Vladimir Crasneanscki quote: “We are delighted to announce this partnership; this is the largest single client deal in the history of our laundry division and demonstrates the growing demand for our services.” • Share price reaction in the piece: +3.7% to 147.00p (Thursday morning).

summary: Great Portland Estates fully lets SIX St Andrew Street at premium rates to AI company
body: Time Period Completion of a leasing campaign (at SIX St Andrew Street, Farringdon) • Let the final remaining office space at SIX St Andrew Street (Farringdon, London) — 3rd and 4th floors, totaling >11,680 sq ft — to an AI company. • Building now fully occupied and generating >£8.8m annual rent at an average of £200/sq ft. • Letting metrics: rent level stated as 6.2% ahead of ERV and 6.2% yield on cost. • Portfolio mix datapoint: AI-related customers represent 11.7% of total office portfolio, or 25.2% of fully managed offices. • GPE quote: “This letting reflects the strong momentum in GPE's fully managed portfolio, where leasing activity accelerated during the final quarter of the financial year… with 19 deals completed and a further seven currently under offer.” • Molly Maguire (Senior Flex Leasing manager) quote: “Completing the leasing campaign at SIX marks a great milestone for our Fully Managed portfolio. Demand continues to be driven by customers looking for high quality, flexible workspace in well-connected central locations, and SIX has proven to be an excellent example of how that proposition resonates.” • Share price move in the piece: -0.9% to 305.80p (Thursday morning).

summary: LGI Homes launches Topaz at Skye Canyon, a 49-homesite townhome community starting at $380s in Las Vegas
body: Time Period N/A (community launch announcement) • Announced grand opening of Topaz at Skye Canyon, a new townhome community within the Skye Canyon development in Las Vegas. • Community size: 49 homesites. • Pricing: homes start from the “$380s.” • Product range: 3- and 4-bedroom townhome floor plans with attached two-car garages. • Four floor plans disclosed with sizes: 1,256 / 1,432 / 1,533 / 1,665 sq ft. • Includes “CompleteHome Plus™” upgrade package (e.g., energy-efficient Whirlpool stainless steel appliances, 42” wood cabinetry, tile backsplash, designer finishes). • Quote (Ron Christian, VP Operations – Nevada): “Expanding into Skye Canyon is an exciting opportunity for LGI Homes… It’s the perfect combination of new construction and a proven community setting where homeowners can feel at home from the very first day.”

summary: Blackbird integrates Epidemic Sound's royalty-free music library directly into its video editor platform
body: Time Period Current product launch (no financial period specified). Launched integrated royalty-free music from Epidemic Sound inside (Blackbird’s browser-based collaborative video editor). All users can now access Epidemic Sound’s catalogue directly within the editor (search/preview/add tracks without leaving the workflow). Licensing is handled at export, and can be tailored to user requirements. Chair Ian McDonough quote: “Music is fundamental to great storytelling, it's what gives video content its emotion and energy. So rather than sending creators off to find tracks elsewhere, we've built it straight into the platform. World-class music, right there in users' workflows. No interruptions, just creativity and complete peace of mind. This is a huge moment for the company and we're incredibly excited.”

summary: West Coast Silver to drill 1,500 metres at Elizabeth Hill seeking high-grade silver extensions
body: Time Period Drilling early April 2026; assay results expected progressively through Q2 2026. JV partner West Coast Silver Ltd plans to start a 1,500-metre diamond drilling programme in early April at Elizabeth Hill Silver Project (near Karratha, Pilbara, Western Australia). Drill programme expected duration: 4–6 weeks. Assay results expected progressively throughout Q2. Drilling focus: test for extensions of mineralisation along strike of the historic Elizabeth Hill high-grade underground silver mine, plus structural targets along the Munni Munni Fault. Chair Bruce Garlick quote: “Our previous 2025 drilling confirmed that silver mineralisation at Elizabeth Hill extends beyond the historical underground mine and associated with a broader structural corridor along the Munni Munni Fault. This current program of diamond drilling below Elizabeth Hill is designed to determine whether there are high-grade silver extensions below and south of the mine. The drilling will also test whether high-grade mineralisation repeats at depth; a model that, if confirmed, could materially expand the resource potential.”

company: GreenTech Metals Ltd (JV partner update relevant to Alien Metals’ Munni Munni exposure)
summary: GreenTech identifies material upside potential at Munni Munni beyond historic mineral resource estimate
body: Time Period Current exploration update (no financial period specified). GreenTech reports “material upside potential” at Munni Munni Platinum-Palladium-Copper-Nickel Project not captured in the historic mineral resource estimate. Historic MRE cited: 24 million tonnes @ 2.9 g/t (Pt, Pd, Rh & Au) for 2.2 million ounces total. Upside items highlighted: high-grade PGE zones above 4 g/t. Upside items highlighted: under-recognised copper and nickel mineralisation outside reef domains. Upside items highlighted: near-surface eastern mineralisation that may be suitable for open-pit mining.

summary: IG Group plans African mineral processing hubs targeting EU and US critical mineral demand
body: Time Period Goals for the next 12 months. Targets launch of multiple processing hubs across Tanzania, Africa, and Latin America. Plans to scale mineral aggregation network via new artisanal & small-scale miners and semi-industrial partnerships. Intends to secure long-term agreements aligned with EU and US critical mineral requirements. Plans to progress ventures on high-scale assets confirmed through rapid validation protocols. Company comment quote: “The synergy between Amigo's visionary leadership and aggressive operational execution has created a platform of Tier-One potential. By controlling the processing nodes and the information layer of the African mineral economy, we are delivering a new standard of transparency and growth. We stand at a pivotal inflection point, uniquely positioned to capture the multi-billion-dollar industrial upside of African mineral beneficiation.”

summary: Crystal Amber Fund restarts share buyback programme for 2.7 million shares worth GBP3.0 million
body: Time Period Buyback restart (no financial period specified). Restarted share buyback programme. Intends to repurchase 2.7 million shares worth GBP3.0 million under the authorisation granted on 2 December. Notes there is no guarantee the programme will be implemented in full.

summary: Eden Research's February placing conditions met following EIS/VCT legislative changes
body: Time Period Placing conditions now satisfied (placing announced in February). Confirms all conditions relating to its conditional placing are now satisfied. February placing raised GBP7.6 million gross at 4.0p per share. Conditionality referenced: in part dependent on EIS/VCT legislative changes (announced in the UK Government Autumn Statement) passing into law.

summary: Quantum Helium begins extended production test at Sagebrush-1 well in Colorado
body: Time Period Extended production test to run “over the coming weeks”. Began an extended production test at Sagebrush-1 well (Colorado). Testing expected to run over the coming weeks; results to be announced after analysis is completed. CEO Howard McLaughlin quote: “Commencing the extended production test at Sagebrush-1 is a major milestone for Quantum and an important step forward for the company. This well has already demonstrated the presence of helium, and this programme is focused on confirming flow rates and commercial potential. The results will be critical in moving our prospective resources towards reserves and defining the pathway to development.”

summary: Guardian Metal Resources received notice of 300,000 share options exercise raising £42,000
body: Time Period Option exercise notice (no financial period specified). Received notice of the exercise of options over 300,000 shares, raising GBP42,000. Exercise price stated as 0.14p per share; note this appears arithmetically inconsistent with GBP42,000 proceeds (GBP42,000 over 300,000 shares implies 14p per share).

summary: Tullow Oil secured consent for restructuring USD1.29bn notes due 2026
body: Time Period Consent solicitation for notes due 2026 (market data referenced “Wednesday afternoon”). Received requisite consents in a consent solicitation related to USD1.29bn 10.25% senior secured notes due 2026. Brent oil referenced at USD95.20/bbl (Wednesday afternoon) vs USD110.24/bbl around the same time the prior day (-13.6% day-on-day). Current stock price referenced: 12.46p, -3.7% on Wednesday; 12-month -8.9%.

summary: Jubilee Metals shareholders approve capital reduction to boost distributable reserves for dividends and buybacks
body: Time Period General Meeting outcome (reported “on Wednesday”). Shareholders approved proposed capital reduction plan. Rationale stated: restructure balance sheet to increase distributable reserves to support potential future dividends and share buybacks. Notes that a resolution to grant directors authority to allot shares was withdrawn. Share prices referenced: Johannesburg 74c (+4.2%) with 12-month -1.3%; London 3.23p (+1.1%) with 12-month +14%.

summary: Rebecca Papp sold 4,033 Next shares worth £521,265 at £129.25 each
body: Time Period Director/PCA dealing (announcement day) • Rebecca Papp (person closely associated with Group Merchandise & Operations Director Richard Papp) sold 4,033 shares at £129.25 each. • Total value of sale: £521,265. • “Current share price” cited: 13,305p (≈ £133.05), implying the cited market price is ~+2.9% above the disclosed sale price. • “12-month change” cited: +21%.

summary: Shell Q1 2026 guidance shows mixed results amid Middle East tensions and production declines
body: Time Period Q1 2026 trading update (guidance ranges) • Integrated Gas output expected 880k–920k boepd in Q1, down from 948k boepd in Q4 2025 (midpoint implies ~-5.1% QoQ). • Integrated Gas guidance reduced vs prior range 920k–980k boepd (new lower end -40k / ~-4.3% vs old lower end; new upper end -60k / ~-6.1% vs old upper end). • Shell attributes Integrated Gas weakness to “the impact of the Middle East conflict on Qatari volumes” and flags: “in light of the ongoing situation in the Middle East, the outlook provided is subject to increased uncertainty.” • LNG volume guidance nudged up to 7.6–8.0mt from 7.4–8.0mt (lower end +0.2mt / ~+2.7%); Q4 LNG volumes were 7.8mt. • LNG drivers: “ramp-up of LNG Canada” offset by “Australia weather constraints” and “Qatar LNG outages.” • Upstream production expected 1.76–1.86m boepd, down from 1.89m boepd in Q4 (midpoint implies ~-4.2% QoQ); includes reduced production after the Adura JV incorporation. • Chemicals & Products indicative refining margin: $17/bbl vs $14/bbl in Q4 (+21.4% QoQ). • Trading statement language: “trading & optimisation is expected to be significantly higher” than Q4. • Balance sheet: non-cash net debt expected to be impacted by a $3bn–$4bn increase in “variable components of long-term shipping leases.” • Working capital expected to “swing” to negative $10m–$15m in Q1 vs positive $1.3bn in the prior quarter (as stated in the article). • Market/analyst colour included in the piece: AJ Bell describes a “dichotomy” where higher energy prices help profits but Middle East footprint disrupts operations; JPMorgan flags a pricing vs balance sheet “timing mis-match” and a “heavy… working capital build.” • Share price / macro context cited: Shell shares -5.3% to 3,377.70p; Brent crude cited at $93.76/bbl vs $110.24 at prior London close (about -15.0%) after the US and Iran agreed a two-week ceasefire.

summary: Close Brothers estimates £320m FCA motor finance redress cost, manageable within capital resources
body: Time Period FCA motor finance redress scheme update (current) • FCA scheme scope cited: 12.1m agreements eligible vs 14.2m at consultation stage (reduction of 2.1m / ~-14.8%). • FCA scheme cost cited: ~£7.5bn compensation; ~£9.1bn total incl. admin (vs earlier projections up to £11bn). • Close Brothers estimates final scheme cost at around £320m, described as “broadly similar” to existing IAS 37 provision of £294m (delta +£26m / ~+8.8%). • Close Brothers language: cost can be “comfortably absorbed” by existing capital resources, leaving it “well positioned” to keep “delivering its strategy.” • Provisioning status: “At this stage, no provision changes have been recognised. The group's existing IAS 37 provision remains under review,” and it will monitor further legal/regulatory/industry developments and consider next steps. • Capital impact: estimate would trim CET1 by ~25bps to 14.0% pro forma; remains above medium-term target 12%–13%. • UBS takeaways in the piece: outcome “reduces potential downside risks”; highlights that FirstRand increased provisions and that Viceroy Research argued Close Brothers would need to at least double provisions. • UBS sensitivity detail (as reported): the £320m estimate assumes 75% scheme take-up; each 5% change = £18m impact and 17bps CET1 impact. • Panmure Liberum scenario (as reported): assuming 100% take-up and excluding a £16m time value discount implies total cost around £426m, and the broker still expects sufficient CET1 headroom. • Shore Capital action (as reported): cut to ‘hold’ with “Take profits,” noting shares up 33% since its upgrade; warns “underlying operational performance remains weak” and “significant execution will be required.” • Share moves cited across the two wires: +16% to 451.54p in the longer “EXTRA” story; +18% to 457.94p in the shorter item.

summary: Greatland Resources posts strong Q3 cash generation and raises full-year gold production guidance
body: Time Period Quarter ended March 2026 (FY Q3) + YTD FY2026 • Q3 production: 82,723 oz gold and 4,128 tonnes copper. • YTD production: 249,887 oz gold and 11.022 tonnes copper (as stated). • Q3 sales exceeded production: sold 97,800 oz gold and 4,620 tonnes copper (vs produced 82,723 oz / 4,128t). • YTD sales: 252,212 oz gold and 10.333 tonnes copper (as stated). • Cash build: “approximately $260m” in the quarter after capex and a $73m tax payment. • Balance sheet: $1.21bn cash and no debt at 31 March, up from $948m at end-2025 (increase $262m / ~+27.6%). • Guidance: now expects full-year production “around or slightly above” the upper end of prior guidance 260k–310k oz gold. • Operational note: Telfer not impacted by diesel supply disruptions linked to Middle East war because plant largely powered by onsite natural-gas generation; diesel used only for highest-grade ore sources. • Hedging note: retains full upside exposure to gold price with partial downside protection from put options. • Share move cited: +14% to 762.50p.
